members.name: enableCachedParameters
members.description: Get or set caching parameter values instead of always evaluating.

members.name: enableOptimizedExprs
members.description: Get or set if Python expression optimization is enabled. Defaults to True every time TouchDesigner starts.

members.name: logExtensionCompiles
members.description: Get or set if extra messages for starting and ending compiling extensions is sent to the textport. Additional error stack will be printed if compilation fails. Defaults to False every time TouchDesigner starts.

members.name: power
members.description: Get or set the overall processing state of the process. When True, processing is enabled. When False processing is halted. This is identical to pressing the power button on the main interface. This has a greater effect than simply pausing or stopping the playbar.

members.name: recentFiles
members.description: Get or set the list of most recently saved or loaded files.

methods.name: addNonCommercialLimit
methods.description: Limits the application to operate at non-commercial license level. Multiple calls can be made, but each can be undone with a matching removeNonCommercialLimit(password). If the password is blank the operation cannot be undone. (See also

methods.name: removeNonCommercialLimit
methods.description: Removes the restriction previously added. Returns True if successful.

methods.name: addResolutionLimit
methods.description: Limits all textures to the specified amount. Multiple calls can be made, but each can be undone with a matching removeResolutionLimit(password). The final resolution limit will be the minimum of all calls. If the password is blank the operation cannot be undone.

methods.name: removeResolutionLimit
methods.description: Removes the restriction previously added. Returns True if successful.

methods.description: Returns the determinant of the matrix.

methods.name: mapUnitSquareToQuad
methods.signature: mapUnitSquareToQuad(blX, blY, brX, brY, tlX, tlY, trX, trY)
methods.description: Set the matrix to be a projection matrix that maps coordinates from to a unit square (0,0) -> (1,1) space to a space defined by an arbitrary quadrilateral (blX, blY) -> (trX, trY). The 4 corners of the quadrilateral are given ('bl' means bottom left, 'tr' means top right etc.).

methods.name: mapQuadToUnitSquare
methods.signature: mapQuadToUnitSquare(blX, blY, brX, brY, tlX, tlY, trX, trY)
methods.description: Is the inverse of mapUnitSquareToQuad(). Mapping coordinates in an arbitrary quadrilateral into a space defined by the unit square.

methods.name: fillTable
methods.description: Fill in the contents of a table from the matrix which the method is called upon.

methods.name: numpyArray
methods.description: Returns this matrix as a 4x4 NumPy array.

methods.name: identity
methods.description: Replaces the values in the matrix with the

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Returns a new matrix that is a copy of the matrix.

methods.name: translate
methods.description: Multiplies the current matrix by a new translation matrix created from tx, ty and tz. The translation is applied from the left of the matrix by default. That is to say, if T is the new translation matrix, and M is the current matrix, then the result of this operation is M = T * M.

methods.name: rotate
methods.description: Multiplies the current matrix by 3 rotation matrices, first a rotation around the X axis by rx degrees, followed by a rotation around the Y axis by ry degrees, followed by the same for rz. The rotation values are in degrees. The rotation is applied from the left of the matrix by default. So if M is the current matrix, then the result of this operation is M = RZ * RY * RX * M.

methods.name: rotateOnAxis
methods.description: Multiplies the current matrix by a new rotation matrix created by rotation angle degrees around the axis specified by rotationAxis. The angle is in degrees. The rotation is applied from the left of the matrix by default. That is to say, if R is the new rotation matrix specified by rotationAxis and angle, and M is the current matrix, then the result of this operation is M = R * M.

methods.name: scale
methods.description: Multiplies the current matrix by a scale matrix created from sx, sy and sz. The scale is applied from the left of the matrix by default. That is to say, if S is the new scale matrix, and M is the current matrix, then the result of this operation is M = S * M.

methods.name: lookat
methods.description: Multiplies the current matrix by a lookat matrix created using the given values to the matrix. The lookat matrix is applied from the left of the matrix by default. That is to say, if L is the new lookat matrix, and M is the current matrix, then the result of this operation is M = L * M. The values for to parameters can be given as anything that can be treated as a list of 3 values. E.g a TDU.Vector, TDU.Position or simply a list of size 3.

methods.name: decompose
methods.description: Decomposes the matrix into its scale, rotate and translate values. These are the same as the translate, rotate and scale that are in the

methods.name: projectionFrustum
methods.signature: projectionFrustum(left, right, bottom, top, near, far)
methods.description: Replaces the contents of the matrix with a projection matrix using the given frustum extents. The left, right, bottom, top extents are located on the near plane. The depth range generated by this matrix will be [0,1] from near to far, as is required by Vulkan.

methods.name: projectionFovX
methods.description: Replaces the contents of the matrix with a projection matrix defined by the FOV(given in degrees), an aspect ratio and near/far planes. The depth range generated by this matrix will be [0,1] from near to far, as is required by Vulkan.

methods.name: projectAndClip
methods.description: Applies a projection matrix to a position if it's within the normalized clip space. Returns None if projected position is clipped.

methods.name: projectionStereo
methods.signature: projectionStereo(ipd : float, convergeZ : float, fovX : float, aspectX : float, aspectY : float, near : float, far : float, rightEye : bool=False)
methods.description: Replaces the contents of the matrix with an asymetrical projection matrix suitable for stereo rendering. The left eye's projection matrix is given by default, set rightEye=True to get the right eye's instead. For proper rendering, the cameras will also need to be translated in X by -ipd/2 and +ipd/2 for the left and right eyes respectively. The depth range generated by this matrix will be [0,1] from near to far, as is required by Vulkan.

members.name: menuNames
members.description: Get or set a tuple of lists of all possible menu choice names. In the case of non menu parameters, None(s) are returned. Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

members.description: Get or set a tuple of expressions that returns objects with

members.name: min
members.description: Get or set the parameter's numerical minimum values. The parameter's values will be clamped at that minimum if

members.name: mode
members.description: Get or set the parameter's evaluation modes.

members.name: name
members.description: Get or set the parameter's unique name.

members.name: baseName
members.description: Get or set the parameter's base name. This excludes any sequence prefixes, sequence indices or name suffixes. Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

members.name: normMax
members.description: Get or set the parameter's maximum slider values if the parameter is a numerical slider. Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

members.name: normMin
members.description: Get or set the parameter's minimum slider values if the parameter is a numerical slider. Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

members.name: normVal
members.description: Get or set the parameter's values as a normalized slider position. Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

members.name: order
members.description: Get or set the parameter's position on the parameter page. Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

members.name: page
members.description: Get or set the parameter page the custom parameter is part of. Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

members.name: password
members.description: Get or set the parameter's password mode. When True all text is rendered as asterisks. Can only be set on Custom string, int or float parameters. Custom Parameters.

members.name: readOnly
members.description: Get or set the parameter's read only status. When True the parameter cannot be modified through the UI, only scripting.

members.name: startSection
members.description: Get or set the parameter's separator status. When True a visible separator is drawn between this parameter and the ones preceding it. Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

members.name: val
members.description: Get or set the constant values of the parameter only. To get the parameter's current values, regardless of the Parameter Modes (

members.name: size
members.description: Get or set the parameter's vector size.

methods.name: destroy
methods.description: Destroy the parameter referenced by this

methods.name: eval
methods.description: Evaluate a parameter group. This value may be derived by the parameter group's constant value, expression, or export, dependent on its mode.

methods.name: evalExport
methods.description: Evaluate the export portions of a parameter, if it contains any. This will ignore any expressions, etc.

methods.name: evalExpression
methods.description: Evaluate the expression portions of a parameter, if it contains any. This will ignore any exports, etc.

methods.name: evalNorm
methods.description: Similar to eval() but the returns the normalized slider values.

methods.name: evalOPs
methods.description: Evaluate each parameter as a series of operators. This is useful for a custom parameter that specifies a list of operator paths for example.

methods.name: pars
methods.description: Returns a (possibly empty) list of parameter objects that match the pattern.

methods.name: reset
methods.description: Resets the parameter group to its default state.

methods.name: pulse
methods.description: Pulsing sets a parameter to the specific value, cooks the operator, then restores the parameter to its previous value.

methods.name: close
methods.description: Close the peer connection. Returns True if successful. Closing a peer can be useful when implementing HTML server protocols for example.

methods.name: sendBytes
methods.description: Send a sequence of bytes through this connection. No terminators are appended. Note: the message will not be OSC formatted.

methods.name: sendOSC
methods.description: Send an OSC formatted message through this connection. Multiple pairs of address/valueLists arguments to be passed. This method will automatically decide what OSC type to convert the passed values to.

methods.name: send
methods.description: Send a sequence of strings through this connection. Note: the message will not be OSC formatted.

members.name: x
members.description: Get or set the x component of the quaternion.

members.name: y
members.description: Get or set the y component of the quaternion.

members.name: z
members.description: Get or set the z component of the quaternion.

members.name: w
members.description: Get or set the w component of the quaternion.
Applies the rotation of one quaternion to another quaternion.

methods.name: lerp
methods.description: Returns the linear interpolation of the quaternion with another quaternion and an interpolation factor.

methods.name: length
methods.description: Returns the length of the quaternion.

methods.name: cross
methods.description: Returns the cross product of the quaternion and argument.

methods.name: rotate
methods.description: Rotates a vector using the current quaternion. Returns a new vector.

methods.name: slerp
methods.description: Returns the spherical interpolation of the quaternion with another quaternion and an interpolation factor.

methods.name: eulerAngles
methods.description: Returns euler angles in degrees as a tuple (i.e. pitch as x, yaw as y, roll as z) from current quaternion and a rotation order. The 'order' argument can be set to any valid rotation order which by default is set to 'xyz'.

methods.name: fromEuler
methods.description: Returns and set the current quaternion from euler angles in degrees as a 3 inputs argument (i.e. pitch as x, yaw as y, roll as z). The 'order' argument can be set to any valid rotation order which by default is set to 'xyz'.

methods.name: axis
methods.description: Returns the rotation axis vector of the quaternion.

methods.name: dot
methods.description: Returns the dot product of the quaternion and the argument.

methods.name: exp
methods.description: Returns the exponential of the quaternion as a new quaternion.

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Creates a copy of the quaternion with separate values.

methods.name: log
methods.description: Returns the natural logarithm of the current quaternion as a new quaternion.

methods.name: inverse
methods.description: Invert the quaternion in place.

methods.name: angle
methods.description: Returns the rotation angle (in degrees) of the quaternion.

members.name: name
members.description: Get or set the sequence block name parameter value. This is implemented as a specifically named parameter in each block. The name is also used to identify a block by string via Sequence[<block name>].

methods.name: destroy
methods.description: Destroy the block of parameters.

methods.name: rand
methods.description: Return a random value in the range [0.0, 1.0) given the input seed value. That is, it will never return 1.0, but it may return 0.0. For a given seed, it will always return the same random number. The seed does not need to be a number. If the seed is not numeric, it resolves it to its string representation to produce a unique value. In the case of OPs for example, its string representation is a constant path. Thus one can produce a unique random value for each OP which remains the same for that OP each time you reload TouchDesigner.

methods.name: clamp
methods.description: Returns the input value clamped between min and max values. Arguments can be any type that can be compared (float, int, str, etc).

methods.name: remap
methods.description: Returns the input value remapped from the first range to the second.

methods.name: base
methods.description: Returns the beginning portion of the string occurring before any digits. The search begins after the last slash if any are present.

methods.name: digits
methods.description: Returns the numeric value of the last consecutive group of digits in the string, or None if not found. The search begins after the last slash if any are present. The digits do not nessearily need to be at the end of the string.

methods.name: validName
methods.description: Returns a version of the string suitable for an operator name. Converts illegal characters to underscores.

methods.name: validPath
methods.description: Returns a version of the string suitable for an operator path, including slashes. Converts illegal characters to underscores.

methods.name: expand
methods.description: Return a list of the expanded items, following the rules of

methods.name: expandPath
methods.description: Expand the file path, using project.paths, the current folder, and any other relevant information.

methods.name: collapsePath
methods.description: Collapse the file path, using project.paths, the current folder, and any other relevant information.

methods.name: split
methods.description: Return a list from a space separated string, allowing quote delimiters.

methods.name: match
methods.description: Return a subset of inputList, in which each element matches the pattern. Wildcards are supported.

methods.name: forceCrash
methods.description: forces a crash for debugging and crash recovery purposes

methods.name: tryExcept
methods.description: Evaluate the first function (func1). If an exception is raised, return second argument instead. Second argument can be either a function, which will be called, or a final result.

methods.name: isPointCloudFile
methods.description: Check if the input file is a point cloud file. For .exr, the channel headers are checked. Returns true if channels ending in X,Y,Z or x,y,z are found, or if the file was saved out using Movie File Out

methods.name: parSummary
methods.description: Returns a dictionary describing the builtin parameters of the OP class provided.

members.name: fps
members.description: Get or set the framerate (in frames per second) of the Timecode.

members.name: negative
members.description: True if the Timecode is negative, and False otherwise. Always False if the Timecode is following SMPTE standard.

members.name: smpte
members.description: True if the Timecode is SMPTE standard, and False otherwise. SMPTE Timecodes cannot be negative and cannot exceed 24 hours.

members.name: cycle
members.description: Get or set whether the timecode cycles. True if the Timecode cycles when the timecode value reaches the custom length (ie. specified with setLength()). If False then the timecode value will hold the last value (ie. length).

methods.name: setComponents
methods.signature: setComponents(hour, minute, second, frame, negative=False, fps=None)
methods.description: Set the Timecode from individual time components.

methods.name: setString
methods.description: Set the Timecode from a string formated as [-]hh:mm:ss:ff.

methods.name: setTotalFrames
methods.description: Set the Timecode to a single integer value representing the new total frames.

methods.name: setLength
methods.description: Set Timecode to a custom length. Useful in conjunction with countdown.

members.name: clipboard

members.name: dpiBiCubicFilter
members.description: Get or set the global DPI scale filtering mode of TouchDesigner windows. True means bi-cubic, False means linear.

members.name: masterVolume
members.description: Get or set the master audio output volume. A value of 0 is no output, while a value of 1 is full output.

methods.name: copyOPs

methods.name: messageBox

methods.name: refresh
methods.description: Update and redraw all viewports, nodes, UI elements etc immediately. This update is otherwise done once per frame at the end of all script executions. For example, if the current frame is manually changed during a script, a call to refresh will cause all dependent data to update immediately.
